title: pithecellobium jiringa
intro: Pithecellobium jiringa, also known as the Jiringa or Monkey Pod tree, is a tropical tree native to the Philippines and other parts of Southeast Asia. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly its seeds, leaves, and bark, which are used to treat various health conditions such as fever, cough, and rheumatism. The health benefits of Pithecellobium jiringa include its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help to reduce pain and prevent infections. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or infusion, or dried and powdered for use in herbal supplements.
